title: In days before he killed house help, friends saw marked change in doctor
content: South Delhi residents are baffled by the arrest of dermatologist Manish Gupta for allegedly murdering his domestic help. While neighbours saw him as reserved, friends noted behavioral changes and recalled he may have been treated for depression. Gupta was known for his charitable work and professional achievements, making the accusation of violence against a long-time employee difficult to comprehend.

title: Man thrashes wife, tonsures her head; arrested after video surfaces
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Korea district, Chhattisgarh, where a man was arrested for brutally assaulting his wife. He allegedly tonsured her head and forced her to drink urine. The FIR was registered after disturbing videos surfaced online. The woman, who had a love marriage 15 years ago, accused her husband of character assassination and abandonment prior to the attack.

title: Exclusive: Yuvraj Singh to take IPL coaching plunge with Delhi Capitals
content: Former India cricketer Yuvraj Singh is set to join the Delhi Capitals support staff from the 2027 IPL season. This marks his first formal coaching role in the league, building on his extensive mentorship of young players. His hands-on approach and impact on careers has been considered to be highly valued.

title: 'Shameless, ungrateful, & corrupt': Aaditya Thackeray on 6 MPs as party faces split 2.0
content: In his first reaction to the crossover of six of the nine Lok Sabha MPs, who constitute two-thirds of the party's strength in the Lower House, Thackeray Junior said: "Maharashtra won't tolerate this". Calling those who are expected to switch to the Eknath Shinde camp "shameless, ungrateful, and corrupt individuals", the ex-Maharashtra minister said the MPs "shamelessly" sold themselves, staking their "reputation and family names".

title: US has a warning for one of Europe's biggest technology company
content: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has warned ASML, Europe's most valuable tech company, that one of its advanced EUV chipmaking machines may have reached China, breaching US export controls. ASML flatly denies ever shipping EUV systems or components to China, while Washington refuses to show evidence. The standoff threatens ASML's $700 billion monopoly and its China revenue amid escalating US-China chip war tensions.

title: Biden aide funded Wuhan lab to 'manipulate' Covid probes? Gabbard's big claim
content: Former intelligence director Tulsi Gabbard released documents alleging Dr. Anthony Fauci funded gain-of-function research in Wuhan and misled Congress. The documents reportedly show Fauci's role in directing US taxpayer funds and interacting with intelligence officials to suppress lab-leak origins.  These claims emerged during a declassification review, with whistleblowers alleging retaliation.

title: India, Russia jointly developing small, hypersonic BrahMos variants
content: India and Russia are jointly developing advanced BrahMos missile variants, including smaller, lighter, and hypersonic versions. These next-generation missiles will significantly boost India's strike capabilities, offering enhanced precision, reduced costs, and integration with a wider range of platforms like fighter jets and unmanned systems.

title: As Accenture shares fall 20%, Julie Sweet says investors are missing the point
content: Accenture's stock plummeted nearly 20% following fiscal third-quarter results that missed revenue expectations and showed a decline in new bookings. Despite the drop, CEO Julie Sweet urged investors to remain patient, highlighting the company's strong positioning for long-term growth driven by AI adoption and a revised salary structure to offer immediate cash to employees.

title: 'Chor, chor': Eggs thrown at TMC leader Vijay Singh outside West Bengal court
content: TMC leader Vijay Singh faced egg pelting and shouts of "chor, chor" outside Asansol court. This follows a similar incident where former minister Udayan Guha was egged outside Dinhata Police Station after his arrest in an alleged extortion case. BJP cited political violence during Guha's tenure. Guha expressed ignorance about his arrest's reason.

title: Ashirvad Sooryavanshi: Batting all-rounder, fish lover and a 'tabahi' in the making
content: Another youngster from the Sooryavanshi household is beginning to attract attention. Ten-year-old Ashirvad Sooryavanshi, who started playing cricket only six months ago, smashed 103 off 87 balls in his first match for Cricket Academy Tajpur. A right-handed batting all-rounder and fish lover, Ashirvad trains on the same pitches where elder brother Vaibhav honed his skills and dreams of watching him score a century in the UK.

title: One of world’s richest persons & Louis Vuitton CEO on Elon Musk becoming trillionaire
content: Elon Musk has become the world's first trillionaire following SpaceX's historic public listing, with the company's valuation exceeding $2 trillion. Bernard Arnault, CEO of LVMH, acknowledged Musk's dominance in the rocket industry but clarified that the wealth represents company value, not liquid assets. Musk emphasized SpaceX's ambition to make humanity multi-planetary and space travel accessible to all.

title: World's first drone AWACS takes flight: What MQ-9B's new radar means for India
content: Sweden's Saab has teamed up with General Atomics from the US to achieve a major milestone: the successful testing of the MQ-9B drone featuring cutting-edge early warning radar technology. This development could redefine aerial surveillance operations, making it more accessible for countries, particularly India, which face vast border and maritime security challenges.

title: PM Modi’s gifts to French, Slovak leaders showcase India’s cultural heritage
content: Prime Minister Modi presented leaders in France and Slovakia with exquisite Indian heritage gifts. French President Macron received a Kalamkari Mahabharata painting, while Brigitte Macron was gifted a Pochampally stole. Slovak officials were honored with Kashmiri carpets, Dokra art, Himroo textiles, and ancient Ayurvedic texts, showcasing India's rich craftsmanship and intellectual legacy.

title: India's golden treasure: Andhra Pradesh to become country's largest top gold producer
content: Andhra Pradesh is set to become India's leading gold supplier, with significant reserves identified in Kurnool district. Jonnagiri village alone is estimated to hold 50 tonnes of gold. Commercial production has commenced, and further exploration is expected to boost reserves. The state aims to leverage private investment for this substantial mining endeavor.

title: Musk tops Buffett in a day: 7 stats on this week’s number platter
content: Elon Musk's wealth surge highlights a future arriving early.  China's consumer spending shows a wobble, while Indian tech jobs face a squeeze.  However, blue-collar roles are growing, and civil engineering sees a comeback.  India's universities are rising, but China leads.  Meanwhile, India's subsidy bill has tripled, reflecting a complex economic landscape where the future is here, but not for everyone.

title: NDA bags 19 out of 26: How close is the ruling coalition to Rajya Sabha two-third majority
content: The BJP-led NDA has strengthened its Rajya Sabha presence, winning 19 of 26 recent seats to reach 150 members. This brings the alliance closer to a two-thirds majority, with potential further gains from upcoming bypolls and shifts in opposition alliances. However, regional parties and upcoming Uttar Pradesh elections could still influence the balance of power.

title: Mukesh Ambani's Jio files IPO papers; offers 27 crore shares of Rs 10 each
content: Reliance Industries' 49th AGM has once again emerged as a key event for investors, with expectations running high for significant announcements across the group's major businesses.

title: Black rain, smoke over Moscow: Russia-Ukraine trade bombs, drones and missiles as war intensifies
content: Ukraine launched a significant drone attack on a major Moscow oil refinery, causing a large fire and disrupting airport operations. This strike, occurring after pledges of Western support, underscores Kyiv's strategy to pressure Russia's energy infrastructure and escalate the conflict's domestic impact.

title: Renting a house? Ask these 10 questions before signing the agreement
content: Finding a rental home is thrilling, but missing important details before you sign can lead to unexpected expenses. It's vital for renters to inquire about all costs beyond the monthly rent, grasp security deposit rules, and know who will take care of maintenance issues.

title: 'Same people fired bullets on karsevaks': Yogi slams oppn as Ram Mandir SIT probe widens
content: Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath accused opposition parties of hypocrisy regarding Ram Mandir donations, highlighting their past opposition to the temple's construction. He assured devotees of a thorough SIT probe into alleged misappropriation, urging against inflammatory statements and encouraging evidence submission.

title: India's world-class airports and the first-impression challenge facing its cities
content: Many Indian cities struggle to impress travellers from the air, often revealing informal settlements and pollution, unlike the orderly, greener landscapes of Singapore, Bangkok, or Colombo. This stark contrast stems from rapid urbanisation, immense population density, and governance challenges, despite India's economic growth. While infrastructure is improving, the sheer scale of the problem presents a significant hurdle.

title: Delhi HC upholds Centre's Telegram ban order ahead of NEET retest
content: Delhi High Court upheld the Centre's temporary ban on Telegram, citing its potential misuse for exam fraud and misinformation ahead of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination. The government's order, issued under the IT Act, was deemed well-founded. The restriction aims to prevent cheating rackets from exploiting the platform, with the ban in effect until June 22.

title: RSS office attack in Ranchi: Pakistan ISI-backed terror network uncovered
content: Ranchi RSS office attack probe reveals links to Pakistan's ISI-backed Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan. Three arrested individuals allegedly communicated with handlers via encrypted apps, sharing footage of the incident. Two accused reportedly met a Pakistani national in Dubai, where they were allegedly radicalized and agreed to support TTH's anti-India agenda.

title: When is Father's Day? History, significance, origin story, and all you need to know
content: Father's Day, celebrated on the third Sunday of June, honors the vital roles fathers play. Originating in the early 20th century with Sonora Smart Dodd, the day officially became a U.S. observance in 1972. It's a time to express gratitude for their love, sacrifices, and guidance, acknowledging their evolving contributions to families and society.

title: How my child fell in love with maths, and why it had nothing to do with talent,
content: A recurring math exam dream highlights the author's past struggles, stemming from a teacher's discouraging words. A new tutor's unwavering belief transformed her performance and confidence. This experience taught her the profound impact of positive reinforcement, a lesson she now applies to her own son, emphasizing that children grow into the expectations adults place upon them.

title: ‘Do you have the guts?’ Sreesanth challenges Harbhajan Singh to ring fight
content: Former India pacer S Sreesanth has challenged Harbhajan Singh to an in-ring fight, reigniting their 2008 'Slapgate' feud. Sreesanth accused Harbhajan of profiting from the incident and urged him to settle their differences through genuine combat rather than advertisements. The challenge comes after a recent ad featuring Harbhajan reportedly reopened old wounds for Sreesanth.

title: US-Iran war impact: India’s Russian oil imports may touch all-time high in June
content: With a US sanctions waiver in place for three months, India stepped up Russian crude oil procurement, and import levels touched near all-time highs. According to analysis of data by Sumit Ritolia, Manager Modelling and Refining at Kpler, India’s crude imports from Russia could hit an all time high of 2.35 million barrels per day in June.

title: 'Praying for his good health & long life': PM Modi wishes Rahul on 56th birthday
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended birthday wishes to Congress MP Rahul Gandhi on his 56th birthday. Several Congress leaders, including Mallikarjun Kharge and Pawan Khera, lauded Gandhi's dedication to constitutional values, social justice, and his resilience in politics. Leaders from Kerala also highlighted his journey and outreach efforts.

title: Faf du Plessis rewrites cricket history, achieves unprecedented feat at 41
content: Faf du Plessis has just redefined age in T20 cricket, setting a record as the oldest centurion at 41 years and 340 days. His remarkable unbeaten score of 104 propelled the Texas Super Kings to a thrilling six-wicket victory over the Seattle Orcas in the electrifying MLC 2026 season debut.

title: Guest hurt during Shimla hotel stay, court orders Rs 20,000 payout
content: A Shimla luxury hotel has been ordered to pay Rs 20,000 compensation to a guest injured on a damaged road. The woman slipped and hurt her foot after the hotel failed to provide promised transport back to her apartment from the restaurant. The consumer commission found the property negligent in ensuring guest safety.

title: Psychiatrist explains why you pick at skin and nails during conversations
content: Unconscious nail biting and skin picking often stem from stress and anxiety, acting as ingrained coping mechanisms. Experts explain these automatic behaviors are triggered by the brain seeking comfort or relief. Self-awareness techniques and keeping hands busy can help, but persistent physical harm or distress necessitates professional intervention for underlying causes.

title: US to withdraw forces from Europe; is this the beginning of Trump's 'pulling the plug' on NATO?
content: The Pentagon is initiating a six-month review to reduce US troop presence in Europe, prompting allies to shoulder more responsibility. Fighter jets, drones, and warships earmarked for European crises will be scaled back, creating gaps NATO members must now fill. This shift emphasizes Europe's need to lead in defense.

title: ‘People switch on TV to watch him’: Srikkanth says Sooryavanshi can revive Test cricket
content: Former India captain Krishnamachari Srikkanth believes teenage batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi could spearhead a Test cricket revival if he realises his vast potential.  Srikkanth urged patience, highlighting the 15-year-old's dominant style and unique aura, stating he is "simply outstanding for Indian cricket" and "the son of God" in cricketing terms.

title: US gas dips below $4 again, yet household budgets remain under pressure
content: US drivers are finally seeing relief as average gas prices drop below $4 a gallon, a welcome change after months of increases. This dip follows a tentative peace agreement between the US and Iran, reopening oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. Despite this, fuel costs remain significantly higher than pre-war levels, impacting household budgets and broader spending.

title: Watch: India’s first LNG carrier reaches Gujarat as Hormuz shipping resumes
content: The LNG carrier Disha has successfully arrived at Dahej Port in Gujarat, heralding a much-anticipated return of maritime activities through the Strait of Hormuz amidst recent tensions in the West Asia region. This docking comes on the heels of a historic US-Iran peace accord, suggesting a potential stabilization of India's vital energy supply lines.

title: Stokes likely to return as captain for 3rd NZ Test despite ongoing investigation
content: England captain Ben Stokes might return for the third Test against New Zealand as investigations into a nightclub incident near conclusion.  Optimism is growing within the team for his comeback.  The ECB has cleared him for competitive cricket, with a decision on his immediate future pending the Cricket Regulator's findings.

title: 'Haven't learnt that lesson yet': Trump denies limits to his power after Iran war
content: President Trump declared no limits to U.S. power after a peace agreement with Iran, digitally signed to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Despite critics noting a retreat from initial demands, Trump insisted the deal represented a military victory and demonstrated American strength, not limitations.

title: 'Party will split': Rajbhar claims Akhilesh's 'real uncle' will take over SP
content: Uttar Pradesh minister Om Prakash Rajbhar launched a sharp attack on Samajwadi Party leader Ram Gopal Yadav, accusing him of casteist remarks and predicting a party split. Rajbhar claimed Yadav viewed Rajbhar and Maurya communities as inferior to Yadavs.

title: Wife can't be forced into narco test: HC rejects plea seeking tests to rebut allegation
content: Rajasthan High Court dismissed a husband's late plea for narco-analysis, polygraph, and DNA tests in a divorce case. The court ruled the application was filed too late, after evidence closed, and was unnecessary to decide the wife's allegations of sexual incapacity. Spouses cannot be forced into such tests without consent.

title: Tamil Nadu passes resolution against Karnataka’s Mekedatu dam project
content: Tamil Nadu's Assembly unanimously passed a resolution urging the Union government to deny all clearances for Karnataka's Mekedatu dam project. The resolution cited violations of tribunal and Supreme Court orders, emphasizing the Cauvery basin's deficit status and the need for concurrence from basin states and the Union government before any new water storage projects are undertaken.

title: T.N. Assembly unanimously adopts resolution against Karnataka government’s Mekedatu dam proposal
content: The resolution urged the Union government not to grant any form of approval, including technical and environmental clearances, to the Mekedatu project

title: Supreme Court declares right to walk on footpath a fundamental right, wants law
content: Apex court says walking powered the freedom struggle, politics and social reforms, but is yet to be recognised as a fundamental right; asks government to frame a law to protect pedestrians

title: ‘Savukku’ Shankar moves Madras High Court seeking CBI probe against former Chennai Police Commissioner A. Arun
content: Says the Vigilance Commissioner failed to take action against the officer despite adverse findings and observations made by the court against him on May 29, 2026

title: Kerala Budget: Private investment the only way to boost State’s economy, says Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan
content: He dismisses Opposition allegations that the UDF is promoting privatisation and says that the government intends to introduce new economic models.

title: Amaravati quantum facility cools home-built refrigerator to -269°C
content: The State government said the cooling marked the first major milestone for its quantum hardware facility at Medha Towers

title: ‘No option left’: Sena (UBT) MP Omraje Nimbalkar breaks silence amid ‘Operation Tiger’ row
content: Speaking with The Hindu from Pune, the MP said he felt torn and that he did not think he was left with any other alternative

title: Jharkhand RS polls: Cross voting allegations ‘unwarranted’, CPI(ML) Liberation leader writes to Congress chief Kharge
content: Jharkhand Congress in-charge K. Raju had blamed Rashtriya Janata Dal and CPI (ML) Liberation for the Congress candidate’s defeat in the Rajya Sabha polls

title: Karnataka’s fight against suicide moves from hospitals to communities
content: As Karnataka grapples with one of the country’s highest suicide burdens, emerging evidence from NIMHANS-led initiatives suggests that prevention may lie not only in hospitals and psychiatric clinics, but also in schools, panchayats, primary health centres and everyday community networks. Afshan Yasmeen recounts how the initative, set to be scaled up across the State from July, works on the ground

title: Kerala Revised Budget Highlights: V.D. Satheesan's Budget pledges 'New Kerala' with inclusive growth, sustainable development
content: Chief Minister says the government is committed to stimulating the economy through broad-based development strategies, attracting large-scale investments across various sectors, mobilising financial resources for the public exchequer, and ensuring the equitable distribution of wealth

title: Order CBI inquiry into DSC recruitment lapses, YSRCP tells Governor
content: A delegation led by Botcha Satyanarayana submitted a memorandum to the Governor, listing five issues, including the question papers and the sports quota

title: SC flags ‘brain drain’ in legal profession, directs creation of fund for young lawyers
content: The top court observed that the lack of a steady client base and limited remuneration in the early years of practice often result in acute financial hardship

title: Conventional warfare and means relevant even today as they were in 1947, says Rajnath Singh
content: The Defence Minister noted that India's defence production surged to more than ₹1,78,000 crore in 2025-26, which was just ₹46,000 crore in 2014

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE | India facing 41% rainfall deficit as southwest monsoon stalls over Maharashtra
content: IMD's region-wise departure rainfall map shows that rainfall deficits in central India, east and northeast India, the southern peninsula, and northwest India stand at 67%, 42%, 22%, and 6%, respectively

title: Kerala Budget announces anti-ragging law, student distress app in memory of J.S. Sidharthan
content: Satheesan says ragging and campus violence continue to be major issues in colleges across the State and that such tendencies also act as barriers to student admissions

title: India calls Pakistan a ‘Frankenstein state' at U.N.; accuses it of supporting terrorism
content: “India is compelled to exercise this right of reply in response to references made to it by Pakistan and the OIC. We categorically reject the baseless and malicious allegations made by Pakistan,” said diplomat Anupama Singh

title: Visakhapatnam Collector inspects fertiliser outlets, electoral roll revision in Bheemunipatnam
content: M. Abhishikth Kishore reviewed urea distribution, the Special Intensive Revision of voter rolls and a school’s AI lab during the visit

title: Kerala Budget earmarks record outlay of ₹5,952 crore for road renovation
content: A ‘Road Architecture Wing’ will be formed to upgrade and modernise the roads, bypasses and junctions in Kerala to international standards

title: Dream projects feature high in Kerala’s Revised Budget; Satheesan announces Land Reforms 2.0, hike in rubber MSP
content: A glut of announcements marks Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan’s 98-minute presentation of Kerala Budget which promises ‘governance with empathy’

title: Great Nicobar project: Jairam Ramesh says Environment Minister Yadav’s Nicobar defence ‘disappointing’
content: Mr. Ramesh has repeatedly challenged the adequacy of Nicobar project’s environmental impact assessments and the secrecy around the High-Powered Committee (HPC) report on the proposed transshipment port’s coastal regulation status.

title: Great Nicobar project is purely commercial with no military infrastructure element: Congress
content: Jairam Ramesh accused the Centre of pursuing what he termed a policy of “Continuing, Calibrated Capitulation to China”

title: North Andhra gaining from data centres, Bhogapuram airport: Rammohan Naidu
content: The Union Minister said the projects would bring jobs to the region, at a Srikakulam meeting marking the government’s two years

title: Shiv Sena (UBT) issues show cause notices to six MPs for failing to attend parliamentary board meet
content: The action comes amidst speculation that the party is set to split again; one of the MPs, Omraje Nimbalkar, still undecided; rebels to meet Shiv Sena chief Eknath Shinde on June 21, but merger unlikely this week

title: Delhi CM directs strict enforcement of ‘No PUC, No fuel’ rule
content: Ms. Gupta said the government is committed to ensuring a clean, healthy environment and improving Delhi’s air quality through sustained and comprehensive measures

title: 204 of 238 Indian cities did not meet air quality standards: CREA
content: Ghaziabad was the most polluted city, recording an average PM2.5 concentration of 172 g/m³, followed by Noida (166 g/m³), and Delhi (163 g/m³), as per CREA, a Finland-based independent research group.

title: Gade Sai Krishna case: Krishnalanka inspector booked for murder
content: The case names Circle Inspector Nagaraju under murder and wrongful confinement, 41 days after the youth was allegedly taken into custody

title: Kerala Budget charts plan to make State a leading ‘unified Port City’
content: Budget proposes harnessing the integrated strengths of the State’s 600-km coastline, 17 non-major ports, two major ports, and its extensive road, rail, sea, and inland waterway networks

title: Kerala Budget: Fiscal health, welfare, growth in focus as Congress-led UDF govt to present Revised Budget on June 19
content: Budget faces challenge of finding middle ground between re-aligning Kerala’s fiscal space for tackling concerns raised in White Paper on State finances and mobilising additional revenue for development and welfare, notably Indira Guarantees and ‘dream projects’

title: Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan to present Revised Budget in Assembly today
content: The budget will also be the first of the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) government which came to power in Kerala in May after a landslide victory in the April 9 Assembly polls

title: Kerala Budget bets on silver economy, launches elderly welfare initiatives
content: Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan says a comprehensive Silver Economic Policy will be formulated and executed, incorporating eldercare services, retirement infrastructure, innovations in geriatric healthcare, and elderly entrepreneurship

title: The controversy over the proposed Mekedatu water project
content: Why is the drinking water project a source of confrontation? Why are both parties unable to come to a settlement?

title: Mekedatu project: Karnataka CM D.K. Shivakumar expresses willingness to talk to Tamil Nadu
content: I will not interfere in their politics. They are building plenty of dams downstream and we have not raised any objection. I am ready to talk with them anytime: DKS

title: Mekedatu dam project: a long-standing source of friction between Tamil Nadu and Karnataka
content: The idea of the project proponent – the Karnataka government – is to impound 67.16 thousand million cubic ft (TMC) of water by building a balancing reservoir at Mekedatu (which, in Kannada, means ‘goat’s leap’), about 100 km from Bengaluru.

title: SC says plea seeking CBI probe into May 13 Tamil Nadu trust vote based on ‘vague’ allegations
content: The top court said the plea was based on “vague, wild and casual allegations” and therefore did not merit interference

title: Kerala Budget aims to attract private investment but makes no mention of PSU projects
content: The total allocation for the industrial sector is ₹1,115.48 crore, while the total allocation for the Industries and Minerals sector is ₹1,558.08 crore

title: Kerala Budget unveils Knowledge Valley, Job Watch Tower, research park to boost higher education
content: The proposed Kerala Knowledge Valley is intended to bring together globally reputed universities, premier Indian institutions, state-of-the-art research parks and centres of excellence under a single ecosystem

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Delhi HC rejects Telegram’s appeal against temporary ban
content: Justice Tejas Karia noted that government’s pro active measures are least restrictive adding that it cannot be held that the order is disproportionate or there was no application of mind before taking the temporary ban decision, as alleged by the petitioner.

title: Kerala Budget: Pinarayi Vijayan says UDF govt ‘rebranded’ LDF schemes, accuses it of promoting corporate interests
content: Former CM and current Opposition Leader says Budget is “largely a renaming exercise” of previously announced schemes without any substantive new direction

title: NEET re-exam: NTA issues safety advisory; sends WhatsApp updates regarding admit cards
content: "NTA is sending reminder SMS and emails to candidates to download their admit cards for the re-examination on Sunday, 21 June 2026," the agency said in a public advisory

title: NEET-PG aspirants anxious over registration delay, NEET-UG cancellation
content: With the exam set for August 30 but the registration date still not announced, students recall two years of postponements

title: Karnataka MLC polls: JD(S) MLA G.T. Deve Gowda dares people to prove he cross-voted
content: Mr. Gowda has been maintaining a distance from the JD(S) during the last few months, and this led to speculation about cross voting.

title: White Paper puts Kerala’s liabilities at ₹5.07-lakh crore, CM says fiscal structure under ‘serious’ strain
content: Salaries, pensions, and interest payments consumed 77.6% of the revenue receipts in 2025-26; a major revamp of KIIFB recommended; urgent reforms sought in KSEB, KSRTC, and KWA; State’s capital expenditure at 1.3% of the GSDP is “one of the lowest” among Indian States

title: Kerala Budget: ‘Land Reforms 2.0’ for reviewing land laws, amending statutes
content: Kerala CM Satheesan announced that a comprehensive ‘land management policy’ would be formulated to address structural challenges in freeing up land for industrial projects

title: Kerala Budget: Decision to slash tax on All India Permit vehicles by 50% expected to reduce off-shore registration of Kerala vehicles
content: According to office-bearers of Luxury Bus Owners Association Kerala, decision will significantly benefit Kerala’s public transport and tourism sectors

title: Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan to present Revised Budget for 2026-27 on June 19, 2026
content: The  presentation of the Budget, the first by the new Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) government, will begin at 9 a.m.

title: Dam over troubled waters: Why the Mekedatu water project continues to divide Karnataka and Tamil Nadu
content: As Karnataka takes further steps towards the realisation of the Mekedatu dam, here is a look at the issue that has been a bone of contention between the two riparian States that have also historically fought over the sharing of Cauvery water

title: CJP’s Dipke writes to PM over ‘crisis of paper leaks’; flags student deaths, appeals for ₹1 crore compensation to families
content: Abhijeet Dipke claimed that 11 students had died by suicide in recent weeks, including five deaths in the last 48 hours

title: BEST employees’ strike continues despite Court order; public bus services hit in Mumbai
content: Among the key demands is the incorporation of contract workers on wet lease into the core service of the undertaking, along with all basic facilities including gratuity

title: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke manhandled, slapped during protest in Jaipur; two youth detained
content: Police detain two youths in connection with the incident; Dipke’s supporters catch hold of the accused and assault them before police intervened

title: The best mangoes in South India, according to farmers who grow them
content: We ask farmers across Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Telangana one question: Which mango would you choose above all others? From Tamil Nadu’s Sappattai to Karnataka’s Appemidi, they reveal the fruits they cherish most — and why preserving heirloom mangoes matters

title: Mortal remains of Patnala Suresh, killed in MT Settebello attack, reach Visakhapatnam
content: Government Whip P.G.V.R. Naidu paid floral tributes to the chief engineer, one of three Indians killed in the June 10 strike off Oman

title: 18 Dead In Israeli Strikes On Lebanon As US-Iran Talks Pushed Back
content: Lebanon's state-run National News Agency reported at least 16 people were killed in Israeli airstrikes, which the Israeli military said were ongoing.

title: "It Could Change In A Week": Expert Explains Why US-Iran Deal Is Tricky
content: For Trump, the details of the peace agreement are politically embarrassing because they suggest a softer approach toward Iran than the administration previously promoted.

title: Ukraine's New Drone Strategy Is Putting Russian Supply Lines Under Pressure
content: Ukraine's latest drone campaign is focused on disrupting the flow of fuel, equipment and personnel behind Russian lines.

title: Hyderabad Court Summons Actor Allu Arjun In Pushpa 2 Stampede Case On June 22
content: Following the charge sheet, filed on December 24, the court issued summons to all 23 accused, including Allu Arjun, directing them to appear before it on June 22.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Slides 600 Points As Accenture Drags IT Stocks
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices edge higher after JD Vance threatened Israel against violating ceasefire.

title: Why Trump Thinks He's 'Most Powerful Person To Walk This Planet'
content: The post, written by a ‘Dave King’, compared Trump’s influence with some of history’s most well-known rulers and military leaders, including William the Conqueror, Genghis Khan, Napoleon and Alexander the Great.

title: A Rare Handshake At LoC Between Officers As Pak National Returns Home
content: Amid ongoing hostility between the two countries, a major rank officer of Indian Army was seen shaking hands with a Pakistan army officer, a rare moment.

title: Top US Scientist Funded Wuhan Lab That Sparked Covid? A Big 'Expose'
content: Tulsi Gabbard, who last month quit as Trump's intelligence chief, said Anthony Fauci funded "dangerous gain-of-function research on bat coronaviruses" at the Wuhan Institute of Virology.

title: US-Iran LIVE Updates: JD Vance Cancels Switzerland Visit, Muted Activity In Hormuz
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Netanyahu pledged that Iran would never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons, regardless of the terms of any agreement.

title: Afghanistan Carries Out Airstrikes In Pakistan Amid Rising Tensions
content: It further claimed that all pre-identified targets were successfully struck and described the operation as a "precise response" to ongoing threats originating beyond its borders.

title: Is The Worst Over? Expert Decodes US-Iran Deal Impact On Inflation, Rupee
content: Aviation, logistics, chemicals, paints, tyres and cement companies are among the sectors that are likely to benefit when fuel prices decline.

title: Fire Breaks Out At Tokyo Elementary School, 300 Students And Staff Evacuated
content: Firefighters rescued one teacher and several schoolchildren out of the building, with non-life-threatening injuries, the department said.

title: "Would Make Patients Lighter": Japan Ex-Doctor Backs Amputations To Ease Care
content: Kusakabe, a former geriatric specialist from Osaka, said removing paralysed limbs would make patients lighter and "reduce the burden on caregivers" in case the care industry reaches crisis point.

title: Explained: How Andy Burnham Could Attempt To Replace Keir Starmer As UK PM
content: Burnham has said he would run in any leadership race, and is the frontrunner to replace Starmer. But some of his supporters are divided over how, and when, to challenge the prime minister.

title: Andy Burnham's 'King Of North' Politics Will Be Tested If He Becomes UK PM
content: After winning a seat in parliament for his local area of Makerfield, Burnham, 56, hopes to ride a level of popularity rarely enjoyed by politicians to become Britain's seventh prime minister in a decade.

title: Sweden PM Ulf Kristersson Faces Fresh Corruption Allegations Ahead Of Polls
content: Headlines across Sweden in recent days have focused on questionable ethics from Kristersson and his wife, ordained Lutheran priest Birgitta Ed.

title: Palestine Urges India, World To Act As Gaza Health System Nears Collapse
content: According to the embassy, more than 12,000 bodies remain trapped beneath rubble across Gaza, while damage to cemeteries has left human remains exposed in several areas.

title: "Higher Parking Fee, WFH": Inside Delhi's Plan To Combat Winter Air Pollution
